title: malvastrum subtriflorum
intro: Malvastrum subtriflorum, also known as the three-flowered mallow, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Malvastrum subtriflorum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: According to Phytotherapy Research: PTR, Malvastrum subtriflorum has been found to possess strong antioxidant activity, among other potential health benefits.
